behaviors.description: The content passed to summary generation includes data from metrics endpoint, issue tracker API, and deployment log.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: yes; a unit test of one collector can miss merge/handoff bugs in the report assembly step. REAL-INFRA: no, this can be validated with in-process fakes/fixtures. MOCK-HIDE: yes for cross-module field mapping/order mistakes. LLM-DEP: no, this checks pre-summary payload composition.
behaviors.test_description: Run the report orchestration with three fixture-backed source adapters and the real payload-building path; assert the exact input object sent to summary generation contains all three sections with expected keys/values and source tags.

behaviors.description: If one source is unavailable, the report is still produced and includes a "data unavailable" note for that section.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: yes; failure is in local error propagation and fallback rendering across modules. REAL-INFRA: not required; source failure can be injected via adapter exception. MOCK-HIDE: unit mocks can miss whether final report composition preserves partial data plus unavailable note. LLM-DEP: no.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end local report assembly with one source adapter raising a timeout/connection exception and two adapters returning data; assert run completes, output contains both successful sections, and failed section is replaced with a standardized "data unavailable" note.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents key values in the input data.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: yes; correctness depends on model output quality/faithfulness, which can vary across runs. MOCK-HIDE: a mocked summarizer would always pass and cannot detect hallucination/omission/paraphrase drift. MULTI-STEP: not necessarily; single model generation + evaluation is sufficient.
behaviors.test_description: Feed controlled report inputs with known critical values into the real summary-generation model/API and assert the produced narrative preserves required facts (e.g., top metrics, issue counts, deployment status) using strict factual checks over multiple trials to detect non-deterministic misrepresentation.

behaviors.description: A complete report flow (collection to email delivery) reaches configured recipient inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: yes; this boundary includes real email transport/network and inbox delivery. MOCK-HIDE: mocked email clients can pass while SMTP/API auth, TLS, routing, or MIME formatting fails in reality. LLM-DEP: not the key failure boundary for inbox arrival.
behaviors.test_description: Run the scheduled job (or equivalent trigger) against reachable test instances/endpoints and a real test mailbox; verify one delivered email appears in the configured recipient inbox with correct subject/body/attachments and expected report content sections.

behaviors.description: If email service is unreachable, save report locally and retry delivery on next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: yes for cross-run state handoff (failed-send artifact -> persisted file -> retry queue/path). REAL-INFRA: no strict need for real network; local failing adapter plus real filesystem and scheduler invocation can catch core logic failures. MOCK-HIDE: pure unit mocks can miss persistence/reload bugs.
behaviors.test_description: First run: force mail sender to fail, assert generated report is persisted to local fallback path with retry metadata. Second scheduled run: restore sender and execute scheduler path, assert saved report is picked up, sent once, and local retry artifact is cleared/marked delivered.
